name: Splenic Tumours (Non-Hemangiosarcoma)
description: The spleen is a common site for tumours in dogs. Nodular hyperplasia (benign) and haematomas account for approximately two-thirds of splenic masses; haemangiosarcoma makes up most malignant cases. All require histopathological diagnosis after splenectomy.
treatment_overview: Splenectomy is the treatment for all sizeable splenic masses. Histopathology determines whether adjuvant chemotherapy is needed. Benign lesions are cured by splenectomy alone.
prevention_tips: Routine abdominal ultrasound in senior large-breed dogs
